differences.title: 1. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Components render on the server, sending minimal/no JS to the client.
differences.rightDesc: Components often ship JavaScript to the browser for interactivity and re-rendering.

differences.title: 2. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Improved perceived performance due to no client-side JS for initial render.
differences.rightDesc: Can have slower initial load times due to JavaScript parsing and execution.

differences.title: 3. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Fetch data directly on the server before sending to the client.
differences.rightDesc: Fetch data in the browser after initial render using hooks or APIs.

differences.title: 4. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Not directly interactive; requires wrapping client components for dynamic behavior.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for rich user interactions, state management, and dynamic UI updates.

differences.title: 5. DEPENDENCIES
differences.leftDesc: Can import Node.js modules and access server-side resources directly.
differences.rightDesc: Limited to browser-compatible JavaScript APIs and client-side libraries.

differences.title: 6. SEO BENEFITS
differences.leftDesc: Search engines easily crawl content as it's rendered server-side.
differences.rightDesc: SEO can be challenging if content relies heavily on client-side JavaScript.

differences.title: 7. RE-RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Re-renders occur on the server. Client fetches updated HTML/data.
differences.rightDesc: Re-renders happen in the browser, often managing client-side state.
